articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Carrasco goal gives experimental Belgium low-key win over Serbia
article.description: Stand-in captain Yannick Carrasco scored in the second minute as Belgium edged Serbia 1-0 in a behind-closed-doors friendly on Wednesday in which both countries fielded fringe players with an eye on next year's European Championship.
article.linkText: Carrasco gives Belgium friendly win over Serbia

images.name: Yannick Carrasco vs. Serbia [1296x729]

images.alt: Belgium's midfielder #11 Yannick Carrasco (L) and Serbia's midfielder #05 Nemanja Maksimovic (R) fight for the ball during the international friendly football match between Belgium and Serbia at the King Power at den Dreef Stadium in Heverlee on November

article.story:
Stand-in captain Yannick Carrasco scored in the second minute as Belgium edged Serbia 1-0 in a behind-closed-doors friendly on Wednesday in which both countries fielded fringe players with an eye on next year's European Championship.

Carrasco took advantage of poor defending to give Belgium an early lead but the hosts were unable to increase their advantage with a virtual second-string side.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Serbia did much the same as they prepared for their last Group G qualifier against Bulgaria on Sunday in which a home win would see them through.

Wednesday's clash was moved to the Den Dreef Stadium in Leuven from Brussels on Tuesday after heavy rainfall left the pitch at the King Baudouin Stadium unplayable. No spectators were allowed as officials said there had not been enough time to arrange adequate security.

Two Swedish supporters were killed by an Islamist militant ahead of a qualifying clash in Brussels last month and the game between Belgium and Sweden was abandoned at halftime.

Belgium, who have qualified for the Euro 2024 finals in Germany, complete their campaign at home to Azerbaijan on Sunday.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Belgium 1, Serbia 0. Yannick Carrasco (Belgium) right footed shot from outside the box to the top right corner.
Substitution, Serbia. Filip Kostic replaces Filip Mladenovic.
Substitution, Serbia. Sasa Lukic replaces Marko Grujic.
Substitution, Belgium. Aster Vranckx replaces Orel Mangala.
Substitution, Belgium. Wout Faes replaces Arthur Theate.
Substitution, Belgium. Dodi Lukébakio replaces Johan Bakayoko.
Milos Veljkovic (Serbia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Belgium. Michy Batshuayi replaces Loïs Openda.
Substitution, Belgium. Leandro Trossard replaces Yannick Carrasco.
Substitution, Serbia. Dusan Tadic replaces Lazar Samardzic.
Substitution, Serbia. Nikola Milenkovic replaces Nemanja Gudelj.
Substitution, Serbia. Aleksandar Mitrovic replaces Dusan Vlahovic.
Substitution, Serbia. Andrija Zivkovic replaces Nemanja Radonjic.
Substitution, Belgium. Timothy Castagne replaces Olivier Deman.
